We build the software your team always needed.

EVOCS delivers architectural-grade technology solutions designed to streamline workflows, eliminate friction, and empower elite teams.

AI Readiness Assessment

This short interactive check helps surface likely gaps.

It is intentionally lighter than the full assessment, which validates these signals against your actual systems, workflows, and data.

Question 1 of 6
Start with your current governance baseline.
1. Do you have a formal AI governance policy or standard today?
2. How are AI use cases evaluated inside the business?
3. Can employees safely query internal company knowledge with AI today?
4. Are AI risks, approvals, and limitations documented in a repeatable way?
5. When do design, product, engineering, and compliance get involved in AI initiatives?
6. Are AI systems monitored after deployment for misuse, drift, privacy, or security issues?

Maturity profile

Governance
Decisioning
Knowledge
Documentation
Collaboration
Monitoring
3.0/5 estimated maturity signal

How It Works

Our consulting engagement turns ambiguous demand into an implementation-ready product path.

We do not start by pushing software. We start by diagnosing where your operational friction lives, then map the right EVOCS solution, the right scope, and the fastest route to value.

Step 1 - Diagnostic and opportunity mappingWe interview stakeholders, review the current workflow, and isolate the process creating the most operational drag, risk, or manual effort.
Step 2 - Solution design for your operating realityWe map the problem to the right EVOCS solution and define the target workflow, integrations, governance model, and rollout boundaries.
Step 3 - Implementation and integrationOur team configures the product, connects your source systems, and turns the proposed workflow into a production-ready operating model.
Step 4 - Enablement, reporting, and optimizationWe hand over the system with training, reporting, and practical next steps so the solution keeps compounding value after go-live.

Recommended Solutions

Depending on your maturity, the right starting point may be governance, identity automation, or safety intelligence.

BARI
Identity & Access Management Platform

A fully automated Joiner-Mover-Leaver engine, software license optimizer, and IT asset management platform.

Day 1 access, guaranteedNew hires active before their start date

One program. Three engines working together.

Most organizations manage identity, licenses, and assets in isolation. The result is delayed onboarding, stale access, over-spend on software, and hardware that disappears when people leave.

BARI replaces all of that with a single, automated platform. Workday drives every decision. Every lifecycle event triggers the right action across every connected system; instantly, with a full audit trail and no manual steps.

The problem

Manual identity management is a liability.

When access is managed by hand, security gaps, wasted spend, and compliance risk are inevitable.

New hires wait days for access

Manual provisioning across every system means new employees spend their first days waiting for access instead of working.

Leavers stay active after they leave

Accounts belonging to departed employees remain active for days or weeks, creating serious security exposure.

Role changes accumulate excess access

Old entitlements are rarely cleaned up. Over time, users accumulate access far beyond what their current role requires.

License spend is invisible

No unified inventory means no one knows how many licenses are owned, allocated, or wasted. Renewals happen at full cost.

Compliance relies on spreadsheets

Without a tamper-resistant audit trail, answering an auditor's question about who had access takes days of manual work.

Assets disappear when people leave

No tracked recovery workflow means hardware walks out the door or remains unaccounted for at audit time.

Capabilities

Three workstreams. One connected platform.

Each engine solves a distinct problem. Together they create a complete identity, license, and asset management system.

Workstream A

Every hire, move, and departure is handled automatically.

Connects directly to Workday. Every hire, role change, or termination triggers provisioning or deprovisioning across all connected systems automatically.

  • Access delta on every role change; prior entitlements removed, new ones granted automatically
  • Day 1 Ready: new hire accounts confirmed active before the start date
  • Same-day leaver deprovisioning across platforms
Workstream B

Stop paying for licenses no one is using.

Inventories every software seat across up to 20 platforms, reclaims dormant licenses automatically, and runs structured recertification campaigns.

  • Full license inventory: entitlements vs. allocations vs. active usage, refreshed nightly
  • Automated reclaim: leaver events and inactivity thresholds trigger instant seat recovery
  • Manager-led recertification: Accept or Decline per user, with campaign tracking and deadline alerts
Workstream C

Every asset tracked, from first assignment to final recovery.

Maintains a single authoritative registry for all hardware and software assets, linked to identity and license records.

  • Single asset registry: every in-scope asset linked to owner identity
  • Joiner fulfilment: hire events automatically generate equipment preparation tasks
  • Leaver recovery: termination events create tracked recovery workflows for every assigned asset
Frequently asked questions

Everything you need to evaluate this program.

Phased across three workstreams. Timeline depends on platform count and policy complexity.

Up to 20 SaaS platforms via API. Core integrations: Microsoft Entra ID, Active Directory, Workday, and ServiceNow.

Validated and imported as a verified baseline. Existing records are linked to identity data before automation goes live.

Contractors follow distinct workflow paths automatically based on worker type derived from Workday attributes.

A structured list of direct reports and their current licenses. One decision per combination: Accept or Decline. Tracked in real time with deadline reminders.

Full documentation, runbooks, and connector configurations are delivered. The platform runs independently after handoff.

Yes. No user role can edit or delete audit records. Every change carries a correlation ID traceable to its Workday event.

It works alongside ServiceNow. Failed provisioning steps automatically create routed tickets and close them when resolved.

Ready to eliminate manual identity management?

INTRODUCING
PIRO
Safety Intelligence Platform

An integrated machine learning platform that transforms EHS data into explainable risk predictions and ranked corrective actions for data center operations.

30 / 60 / 90-day forecastingIncident forecasting across every campus
Deployed in your Azure tenantNo data leaves your environment
Ranked corrective actionsFrom raw incident data to prioritized fixes
piro.evocs.io/dashboard

From Reactive Reporting to Predictive Safety Intelligence.

Most data center EHS teams operate in a reactive cycle: an incident happens, it gets logged, and the investigation begins after the fact. Historical data sits in disconnected systems without a unified way to surface patterns, predict future events, or prioritize where to allocate safety resources.

PIRO replaces that cycle with a predictive intelligence layer. It ingests incident records, near-miss reports, sensor streams, and workforce data into a unified Azure Data Lake, processes them through eight machine learning models, and delivers ranked corrective actions.

The problem

Safety Data Is Everywhere. Safety Intelligence Is Nowhere.

The challenges that keep EHS teams stuck in a reactive cycle; and the risks that come with it.

Incidents are investigated, never predicted

There is no system surfacing risk patterns before they become events.

Risk classification is manual and inconsistent

Safety managers apply their own criteria, creating inconsistent risk tiers across campuses.

Sensor anomalies are detected too late

IoT alerts fire after thresholds are breached, with no predictive layer between sensor trends and incidents.

Near-miss narratives go unanalyzed

Near-miss reports are filed as free text and never processed systematically.

Resource allocation is based on intuition

Safety resources are assigned on gut feel, not data; high-risk areas routinely receive less coverage.

Safety data lives in silos

Incident records, sensor streams, and near-miss logs exist in separate systems with no unified view.

Capabilities

Eight Models. One Integrated Safety Intelligence Pipeline.

Each module solves a specific EHS problem. Together, they form a connected pipeline that moves from raw data to ranked corrective actions.

NLP Intelligence

Extract Structured Insights from Every Incident Narrative.

Processes free-text incident and near-miss reports automatically, extracting entities, severity signals, and sentiment without manual tagging.

  • Entity extraction: location, equipment type, injury category, and contributing factors identified from raw text
  • Severity classification: each report scored and ranked by potential impact
  • Feeds directly into the risk classification and forecasting models
Risk Classification

Classify Every Event by Severity Automatically.

Classifies every event into one of four risk tiers using a gradient boosting model with full SHAP explainability.

  • Four tiers: Critical, High, Medium, and Low, refreshed automatically as new data arrives
  • SHAP values show exactly which factors drove each classification decision
  • Benchmarked against LightGBM and Random Forest; best performer selected per dataset
Incident Forecasting

Forecast Incident Frequency 30, 60, and 90 Days Ahead.

Predicts incident frequency at 30, 60, and 90-day horizons across every campus using walk-forward validated time-series models.

  • Campus-level forecasts updated as new incident data is ingested
  • Confidence intervals provided alongside point estimates
  • Forecast accuracy tracked and reported automatically
Action Recommendations

Deliver Ranked Corrective Actions.

Generates 5–10 ranked corrective actions per risk event using a combination of retrieval-augmented generation and multi-criteria decision analysis.

  • Each recommendation linked to the specific risk factors that triggered it
  • Actions ranked by impact, feasibility, and urgency
  • Direct ServiceNow integration: recommendations create structured tickets automatically
Resource Optimisation

Allocate Safety Resources Where They Matter Most.

Allocates safety resources across campuses, shifts, and teams using a linear optimiser running 50,000 Monte Carlo simulations.

  • Constraints include headcount, shift patterns, equipment availability, and regulatory minimums
  • Latin Hypercube Sampling ensures efficient coverage of the uncertainty space
  • Output is an actionable allocation plan, not just a recommendation

Ready to move from reactive to predictive safety?

OSIMO
Automated User Access Review Platform

A centralized platform that automates periodic user access reviews across your enterprise systems, replacing spreadsheets with real-time compliance visibility.

Centralize up to 100 systemsReduce manual review effort dramatically
Real-time compliance visibilityReview statuses, anomalies, and audit readiness
Automated review workflowsConfigurable notification protocols
osimo.evocs.io/dashboard

One platform for every access review

Most enterprises still run user access reviews through a patchwork of spreadsheets, email threads, and disconnected tools. The result is slow review cycles, inconsistent data, and compliance gaps that surface only during audits.

Osimo replaces that patchwork with a single, centralized system. It connects directly to Active Directory, Azure AD, Workday, Salesforce, ServiceNow, and other in-scope systems; synchronizing user access data in real time, automating periodic review workflows, and delivering audit-ready reports through integrated Power BI dashboards.

The problem

Challenges

The problems your security and compliance teams know too well.

Manual Reviews Drain Productive Hours

Access reviews run on spreadsheets require hundreds of hours per cycle. Reviewers work through lists manually with no automation and no consistency checks.

Fragmented Data Across Disconnected Systems

User access data lives across Active Directory, Workday, Salesforce, and ServiceNow with no unified view. Every review starts with a data reconciliation effort.

Compliance Gaps Surface Only During Audits

Without continuous monitoring, access anomalies and policy violations are only discovered when an auditor asks. By then, the gap already exists on the record.

No Real-Time Visibility into Review Status

There is no live view of which reviews are complete, which are overdue, or which users have flagged access. Status updates require manual chasing.

Inconsistent Processes Across Business Units

Each team runs reviews differently. Some use spreadsheets, some use email, some skip steps entirely. The result is an inconsistent audit trail.

Scaling Reviews Is Unsustainable

As the user base and system count grow, manual review cycles become unmanageable. Adding more reviewers does not solve the underlying process problem.

Capabilities

Three Modules. One Access Intelligence Platform.


Module 1

Integration & User Data

Connects all in-scope enterprise systems into a single repository, synchronizing user access data in real time.

  • Native connectors for Active Directory, Azure AD, Workday, Salesforce, and ServiceNow
  • Real-time sync ensures review data reflects current access state, not a stale export
  • Supports up to 100 enterprise systems and 2,000 AD/AAD groups
Module 2

Access Review Automation

Replaces manual review cycles with configurable automated workflows, role-based approval routing, and escalation logic.

  • Configurable review triggers: periodic, event-driven, or on-demand
  • Role-based routing: each reviewer sees only the access decisions relevant to them
  • Automated escalation and deadline reminders eliminate the need for manual chasing
Module 3

Reporting & Compliance

Delivers real-time compliance dashboards, Power BI integration, and audit-ready reports aligned to ISO 27001 and NIST.

  • Pre-built KPI dashboards: review completion rates, anomalies detected, compliance posture
  • Standardized audit report templates generated automatically
  • Anomaly detection flags unusual access patterns for immediate review
Frequently asked questions

Everything you need to evaluate this program.

A typical deployment runs 15 weeks, covering project initiation, system design, integration, testing, and training. Timeline varies with the number of in-scope systems.

Native connectors for Active Directory, Azure AD, Workday, Salesforce, and ServiceNow. Supports up to 100 enterprise systems and 2,000 AD/AAD groups.

No. The platform layers on top of existing infrastructure via non-invasive integrations. Change management support and training are included.

EVOCS maps and validates existing data sources, then synchronizes records into the platform. Your team confirms accuracy before go-live.

Yes. Configurable review triggers support event-driven workflows for role changes, onboarding, and offboarding. Temporary access can be set to auto-expire.

EVOCS provides 30 days of post-go-live support, then delivers full documentation and knowledge transfer sessions for independent operation.

Full audit trails for every review action, anomaly detection, and alignment with ISO 27001 and NIST. Security is validated during a dedicated testing phase.

Pre-built KPI dashboards and Power BI integration. Real-time views of completion rates, anomalies, and compliance posture. Audit templates generated automatically.

Ready to automate your access reviews?

INTRODUCING
ALBA
The AI platform your enterprise owns.

Running AI everywhere. Governing it nowhere. Alba is the sovereign infrastructure layer that puts every model, interaction, and cost under your control.

halo.evocs.io/platform
The Problem

Your AI is ungoverned.

Enterprise AI doesn't have to be fragmented, risky, or hard to scale.

Enterprise AI doesn't have to be fragmented, risky, or hard to scale. Alba is the sovereign infrastructure layer that puts every model, interaction, and cost under your control.

The Platform

One foundation.
Every AI capability.

For engineering teams

Build AI-powered apps without starting from scratch.

Alba gives developers a single governed API to access any model, manage routing, run RAG pipelines, and deploy agents. Security and full audit trail are built in.

  • Unified API across 11k+ frontier, open, and fine-tuned models
  • Intelligent routing by cost, latency, and task type
  • Built-in RAG pipeline: connect your data sources in days
  • Agent orchestration with full execution audit trail
  • Policy guardrails enforced at the gateway, with no extra code required
For data teams

Run analysis across all your enterprise data.

Alba connects your structured and unstructured data sources into a single queryable layer. Data teams can run analysis, generate reports, and compare model outputs, all within your governed environment.

  • Query structured databases and unstructured documents in one place
  • Compare model outputs side by side for quality and cost
  • Automated report generation with source citations
  • Prompt versioning and evaluation built in
  • Export results with full lineage: model, prompt, and data sources
For every team

One place for all the AI your organization runs.

Any team across sales, operations, HR, and finance can access AI tools built on Alba without touching a line of code. Discover what's available, ask questions, and get answers in plain language.

  • Browse all AI tools and agents built by your team in one hub
  • Ask questions in plain language, no prompting skills needed
  • Personalized workspace per team: sales, ops, HR, finance
  • Every interaction governed, logged, and policy-compliant
  • No new accounts needed. Sign in with your existing company credentials
Benefits

Why enterprises build on Alba.

AI governance and security

Build and Scale AI with Confidence

Centralize guardrails, policy enforcement, auditability, and security controls so teams can adopt AI without creating unmanaged risk.

Compound costs

Every new capability costs less

Reuse the same governed foundation across use cases so each application benefits from the modules, workflows, and infrastructure already in place.

Flexibility

All the models you want

Route across frontier, open, and small language models based on cost, latency, and task fit instead of locking the enterprise into a single option.

FAQ

Common questions.

No. Alba is deployed inside your own Azure environment. You own the infrastructure, the data, and the model interactions. There is no shared cloud and no vendor data access.

Alba supports over 11,000 models including frontier models (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini), open-source models (Llama, Mistral), and fine-tuned variants. You route across them based on cost, latency, and task fit.

Most deployments are production-ready within 6 months from kickoff. EVOCS handles the full implementation: infrastructure, integrations, governance configuration, and team enablement.

Alba includes policy enforcement, role-based access control, tamper-resistant audit logs with 7-year retention, prompt and response filtering, and SLA monitoring, all configurable to your organization's requirements.

Yes. Alba connects to your existing data sources, internal tools, and enterprise systems through a managed integration layer. EVOCS scopes the required integrations during the discovery engagement.

Your enterprise deserves AI it actually controls.

Overview Problem Solution
INTRODUCING
LAGOS
Private Market Intelligence Copilot.

Critical market signals like customer demand, competitor moves, and pricing shifts remain buried across dozens of disconnected systems. Lagos is a secure, Azure-native copilot that synthesizes your proprietary intelligence into sourced, cited answers in under 30 seconds.

lagos.evocs.io/query
The problem

Intelligence exists. Access doesn't.

Your organization generates and consumes large volumes of proprietary market intelligence every day, yet it stays buried, siloed, and out of reach when decisions need to be made.

Data fragmentation

Intelligence is scattered across SharePoint sites, email systems, and regional teams with no unified access point. Executives cannot get a cross-regional view without manually aggregating from multiple sources.

Manual synthesis

Executives spend significant time manually searching, reading, and collating insights from disparate documents before any analysis can begin, consuming time that should be spent on decisions.

Delayed decisions

Time-sensitive competitive intelligence becomes stale before it can be effectively analyzed. By the time insights are assembled, the market opportunity may have passed.

Knowledge silos

Regional insights remain isolated, preventing cross-regional pattern recognition. APAC trends that predict EMEA movement go undetected.

No source traceability

Informal channels and memory replace documented evidence. Executives cannot trace a claim back to its source, reducing confidence and increasing decision risk.

Research burden on teams

Market strategy and regional teams spend significant time fielding executive requests and manually assembling briefing materials instead of producing new intelligence.

The solution

Ask in your own words. Get cited answers in seconds.

Syntethize all your proprietary data corpus into executive-grade insights with mandatory source citations.

Natural language queries. No dashboards to learn.

Any authorized executive types a question in plain English. Lagos retrieves evidence across all indexed sources, including APAC, EMEA, and NA intel trackers, third-party research, and pricing documents, then synthesizes a coherent, structured answer.

Every claim. Every source. Cited.

Lagos's system prompt enforces citation on every factual claim. Every insight is traceable to its source document, eliminating the reliance on memory and informal channels that undermines executive confidence.

Fully private. Zero training exposure.

Lagos runs inside your Azure environment on Azure OpenAI. Your proprietary intelligence is never sent to external model training pipelines. Access is restricted via Microsoft Entra ID SSO and role-based controls.

Overview Problem Solution
INTRODUCING
PONTIX
AI Use Case Intake & Governance Platform.

AI demand consistently outpaces governance capacity. Pontix is the single governed front door for all AI opportunity submissions: a conversational platform that turns scattered ideas into structured, decision-ready business cases.

pontix.evocs.io/intake
How it works

From idea to execution in six steps.

Every AI opportunity goes through a consistent, governed process, so nothing gets lost and every decision is traceable.

1
Submit
Describe your idea in plain language, no templates or forms required
2
Clarify
Pontix asks targeted follow-up questions to fill in the gaps
3
Enrich
Upload supporting docs; RAG pulls relevant context automatically
4
Evaluate
10-dimension scoring model assigns a value and effort classification
5
Route
Decision engine recommends Build, Partner, or Defer
6
Handoff
Approved cases become ClickUp tasks with stakeholder notifications
The problem

AI demand outpaces governance.

Ideas arrive through unstructured channels: emails, slide decks, and hallway conversations, making triage slow, subjective, and opaque.

Unstructured intake

AI opportunities arrive as emails, spreadsheets, and informal conversations. This creates inconsistency in completeness and makes comparison at scale impossible.

Slow, subjective triage

Without a common scoring framework, teams struggle to rank opportunities by value and effort. Decisions are delayed, rework multiplies, and stakeholder confidence erodes.

Scattered evidence

Supporting documentation lives in disconnected systems and can never be systematically incorporated into the evaluation process.

No visibility after submission

Submitters receive no status updates. Ownership and next steps are unclear, producing frustration and duplicate submissions.

No audit trail

Without a governed record, leadership cannot understand the AI opportunity pipeline or justify prioritization decisions to the board.

Good ideas stall in delivery

Even approved use cases lose momentum without a clean handoff. Manual coordination between intake and execution teams delays value realization.

The solution

One governed front door for every AI idea.

Pontix replaces ad-hoc channels with a chat-first interface that guides any business user through submitting a use case, automatically scoring, routing, and handing off to execution.

Conversational intake. No forms.

Any employee describes an AI or data opportunity in plain language. Pontix confirms its understanding, asks targeted follow-up questions, and extracts all required fields automatically — without the submitter filling a single form field.

10-dimension scoring + ROI classification.

Every submission is evaluated across Benefit, Feasibility, and Effort dimensions. Pontix produces an ROI classification (High / Medium / Low) with written rationale, giving governance teams a repeatable, defensible signal to prioritize from.

Explicit routing outcomes.

Governance users assign every submission one of four outcomes: Build, Configure / Optimize, Partner, or Defer. Pontix then sends stakeholder notifications and creates a ClickUp task, so approved work starts immediately.
